codeReview.summary: The compact quest card routing, prioritization, lore fallback, and unit-tested formatting helpers are implemented, but the feature is incomplete in two blocking ways: the card does not display reward items with icons as required, and turn-in from the new card does not grant item rewards even though the old dialogue flow handled them.

issues.description: The card renders rewards as a plain summary string via `formatRewardSummary(...)` and never creates any item icon visuals, so it does not satisfy the requirement to display rewards as exp/gold/items with icons.

On turn-in, the compact quest card only applies exp, gold, pet reward, and achievement updates after `questSystem.turnInQuest(...)`; it never creates or adds `reward.items` to the inventory. This regresses quest reward delivery compared with the existing dialogue reward handling in the same file around the choice reward path (`createItem` + `inventorySystem.addItem`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ui-worker` skill says visual verification in browser is critical for UI work, but mission `AGENTS.md` explicitly says command-only validation and no browser-based validation. The worker followed the mission constraints, so the skill guidance is out of sync with mission reality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/abyssfire/.factory/skills/ui-worker/SKILL.md` section 'Visual verification in browser. This is critical for UI work' vs mission `AGENTS.md` line stating '**NO browser-based validation** — user explicitly opted for command-only validation'.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 34159f6 diff, and relevant UIScene/QuestCardUI code. The feature correctly routes quest NPC interactions to a compact card with prioritization and lore access, but it fails the spec because reward item icons are not shown and turn-in through the new card drops item rewards entirely.
